name: Perm Krai (Russia)

colors.symbolism: courage, bravery and the fearlessness of the people of Perm
the rivers and lakes of the krai and the gentleness of its people
peace, virtue and purity of thought

description: A field divided by a white Greek cross into four quarters: red at the upper hoist and lower fly, blue at the upper fly and lower hoist; the coat of arms of Perm Krai is centred on the cross.
history: Adopted on 7 October 2007 by the Legislative Assembly of Perm Krai, after the merger of Perm Oblast and Komi-Permyak Autonomous Okrug; the design retains the historic Saint George's Cross arrangement of the previous Perm Oblast flag.

features.symbolism: the white cross is the cross of Saint George, patron of Russia; it divides the field into red and blue quarters, the colours of the Russian state.

features.role: coat of arms of Perm Krai
features.symbolism: a walking silver bear bearing a golden Gospel topped by a silver cross on its back, the historic emblem of the Perm land since 1783.
